title: THE EIGHTH PASSENGER — ECHO DIRECTOR'S CUT V7
episodeObjective: Reach the external emergency pod, identify the occupant without turning the scene into report language, make a visible irreversible Noah Voss decision, and carry Maya/Hale/Noah consequences into Episode 09.

summary: Scenario spine preserved: pod discovery, scan/approach, route choice, hatch conflict, Noah identification, extract/seal irreversible branch.
Every visible choice set has exactly two choices.
CharacterBusy durations reduced to preserve real-time pacing.
Report-style lines were rewritten into lived reactions.
Maya and Hale visibly remember the operator's risk/caution choices.
Fast route, careful route, verify/contact, fast/controlled breach, extract/seal all create Episode 09 flags.
UNKNOWN messages now push story instead of generic glitch noise.
Noah/Voss/Eva hooks are preserved, but consequence flags are stronger.
Final extract and seal branches create distinct Episode 09 start states.

e08_n01.speaker: MAYA
e08_n01.objective: Establish the pod as the concrete rescue target.

events.text: The one the archive said would remember.
It's outside the broken relay spine.
Like something grabbed it halfway out.
Tell me you heard that too.

effects.mayaTrustVisible: Maya remembers the operator stopped her from rushing the pod.

choices.text: GUIDE MAYA CLOSER TO THE POD.

effects.mayaTrustVisible: Maya remembers being sent in close before scan data.

events.text: I want to sprint at it.
If I breathe wrong, we lose it.
No static. No ghosts in the numbers.

events.title: POD MANIFEST
events.content:
Occupant: UNKNOWN
Life Support: 23%
Pressure: unstable but contained
Beacon Origin: external emergency pod
Visual Feed: unavailable
Manual Access: possible
Warning: identity not confirmed
Carrier Residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
events.caption: Remote scan confirms an active life-support pod with an unknown occupant.

events.text: Whoever it is doesn't have time.
I can open it. That's the part I hate.
That feels like the station handing me a knife.
Clip's fighting me. Give me a second.
Nothing under my boots except bad decisions.
Close enough to see frost on the hatch.
The pod corrected its drift while I crossed.
Enough to prove it isn't wreckage.
You sent me in close before we knew that.
Physics waiting to make a point.
Third option is waking up somewhere safe.
Fast route. Of course we picked the sharp one.
I'm stepping onto the rib. It is narrower than it looked from back there.
No talking while I cross. If I hear myself panic, I may start agreeing with it.
There's a white scrape across my suit now.
Tether path. Slow and less stupid.
Sensible. I hate how much I appreciate sensible right now.
Handhold first. Then suit line. Then the next breath.
This will take longer. Don't make a speech while I'm gone. I won't hear it.
Or someone dragged something through it.
I hope Noah had minutes to spare.
That pod should not be here.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Like fear in a lab coat.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If the pod corrects its own drift...
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm noticing you picked his fear over mine.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Running it. And trying not to hear her breathing at the hatch.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: POD HATCH INTEGRITY
events.content:
Seal: intact
External frost: fresh
Manual lever: responsive
Motion correction: active
Carrier residue: 7.14 MHz harmonic
Risk: opening may trigger automatic occupant revival sequence
events.caption: Hatch check shows active 7.14 residue and a possible automatic revival sequence.
events.message: [POD HATCH INTEGRITY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That phrase should require a permit.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I can hear it in how quiet she got.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: POD CONTACT WAKE
events.content:
Manual contact detected
Display state: partial wake
Suit registry ping returned
Recovered ID fragment: NV-08
Warning: occupant identity unresolved
events.caption: Manual contact wakes the pod display and returns suit fragment NV-08.
events.message: [POD CONTACT WAKE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Tell me this isn't what I think it is.
Hale is still on the line.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I don't need a puzzle for this.
I need it to not be true.

events.title: DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
Subject: Noah Voss
Status: deceased
Reported Date: before Incident Alpha
Body Recovery: confirmed by Engineering Lead
Witness: Ethan Hale
Conflict: active biometric signal currently transmitting under suit ID NV-08
events.caption: Recovered death record directly conflicts with pod telemetry.
events.message: [DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Hand curled like he was about to argue.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Most days I needed that one more.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm putting the mic against the hull.
No one breathe dramatically. That includes me.
I need silence for this. Real silence.
Like stepping back saved anyone here.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Twenty-three became twenty-one while we argued.
If we delay, whoever is inside dies.
Open it wrong and we risk depressurization.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Or wake whatever signal is riding it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And yes, Hale, I know what you want.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: FAST RESCUE: OPEN THE HATCH NOW.

events.text: I'm releasing the manual latch and pulling the occupant line.
She could have lost the seal.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If something comes through with him, that's on us.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Thank you for choosing the option least likely to turn Maya into a cautionary diagram.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm anchoring the hatch while he calculates pressure release.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Not enough to kill him if the pod kept pressure, but enough to make the universe feel personal.
The faceplate is fogged from the inside.
Inside means I have to look.
Maya, step away from the pod.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Don't you dare make this smaller than it is.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: His eyes are moving under the lids.
Thank you for making me do the thing that doesn't involve staring at a possibly dead friend's face.

events.title: POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE
events.content:
Suit ID: NV-08
Heartbeat: active irregular
Neural response: suppressed
Body temperature: survivable
Identity confidence: 61% Noah Voss
Warning: memory imprint contamination detected
events.caption: Wrist biometrics suggest Noah Voss but warn of memory imprint contamination.
events.message: [POD BIOMETRIC WRIST TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That's not enough to celebrate. It is absolutely enough to ruin me.
There it is. The phrase that makes everything worse.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: the signal may come with him.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Save the body. Risk the signal.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Seal the pod. Keep him alive. Keep him trapped.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Make the call before I make the emotional one.
events.speaker: MAYA

effects.episode09OpeningPressure: Noah is physically present and the signal came with him.

choices.text: SEAL POD AND KEEP HIM ALIVE.

effects.episode09OpeningPressure: Noah remains alive inside the pod and Maya resents the operator.

events.text: I'm going to be furious when you wake up.
The pod screamed through the comms.
I can't process the whole sentence.
I need to get him inside.
Operator, you chose a body over a clean signal.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The whole impossible shape of it.
I can see his breath fogging the faceplate.
I know why we did it.
Do not ask me to like it.
That makes me want to break something.
Operator, you chose a sealed door over a living hand.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: That does not make it smaller.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm hauling him back through the tether.
He is heavier than he looks. Or I am more tired than I want to admit.
I need both hands. I'm moving now.
Every ping makes the lights twitch.
Or brought something in with him.
events.speaker: HALE

choices.text: LET NOAH REST AND MONITOR HIM.

events.text: I'm backing away from the pod.
I keep expecting him to knock from inside.
Noah used to do that on bulkheads when he wanted attention.
Or something in there knows that.
I need to get out of the pod frame before I change my mind.
I'm back at the relay spine.
Like a pulse through a wall.
I keep thinking I should have argued harder.
Then I remember his life support stabilized.
Noah. Stay with me. Look at my hand if you can't look at my face.
Hale just stopped breathing on the channel.
I hate that the right thing can look so much like abandoning someone.
His pulse is ugly but present.
He said one thing before his eyes closed again.
Hale, why does that name make the signal colder?
I keep repeating that because maybe if I say it enough, it becomes a strategy instead of a wound.
The pod is alive. Noah is alive. The channel is not quiet.
We need to find Eva Thorne.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Noah is in the relay bay.
Then we move before it learns the room.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Do not forget that just because we survived it.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: MOVE NOAH TO A SAFE ZONE.

events.text: I am going to hear that forever.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operator, we either find Eva now...
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: or we go back and admit what we left behind.
events.speaker: MAYA

choices.text: LEAVE THE POD AND FIND EVA.

directorCut.coreObjective: Reach and identify the external emergency pod occupant, then make a permanent Noah Voss decision with visible human cost.
majorChanges: CharacterBusy durations reduced from 120-300 seconds to 35-60 seconds.
Report-style dialogue replaced with lived human reactions.
Route choices now carry physical/oxygen consequences into Episode 09.
Fast rescue and controlled breach now create distinct Noah conditions.
Extract/seal branch now explicitly carries irreversible Episode 09 consequences.
UNKNOWN lines rewritten to push story instead of generic atmosphere.
rulesApplied: Characters produce progress even when stationary.
Every visible choice has exactly two options.
Every major choice creates visible dialogue or future flag consequences.
Episode ends with Eva Thorne hook and distinct Episode 09 states.

episode09UsageNotes.podScanFirst: Maya should show more trust in the operator's caution.
episode09UsageNotes.movedCloserEarly: Maya should remember the operator sent her close before confirming pod risk.
episode09UsageNotes.mayaSuitScored: Maya can complain about suit damage and physical stress.
episode09UsageNotes.noahOxygenLostFromDelay: Noah begins weaker if the careful route or controlled breach delayed rescue.
episode09UsageNotes.operatorBackedHaleAtHatch: Maya starts colder; Hale starts more cooperative.
episode09UsageNotes.operatorBackedMayaAtHatch: Maya starts more attached; Hale starts more strained.
episode09UsageNotes.noahSignalContamination: The signal follows Noah into Episode 09 if extracted fast.
episode09UsageNotes.noahSealedInPod: Maya resents the operator and Noah remains present as a trapped living consequence.
episode09UsageNotes.podChannelKeptOpen: UNKNOWN interference should be stronger at Episode 09 opening.
